title: sempervivum marmoreum subsp. ballsii
intro: Sempervivum marmoreum subsp. ballsii, also known as the Ball's houseleek or Ball's hen and chicks, is a rare succulent species native to the British Isles and Ireland. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, as it contains compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Sempervivum marmoreum subsp. ballsii include its potential to soothe wounds, reduce inflammation, and support immune function. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and salves to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
